name: Carrot and Raisin Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Mix cracker curls, sugar, cinnamon, and melted butter. Press mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Bake for 10 minutes.
3. Increase heat to 450°F.
4. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese with 1/2 cup sugar until smooth. Add flour gradually, beating well after each addition. Beat in eggs one at a time, mixing thoroughly after each egg. Stir in orange juice.
5. In another bowl, combine remaining flour, shredded carrots, raisins, nutmeg, and ginger. Mix into the cream cheese mixture until combined.
6. Pour batter over crust.
7. Bake for 10 minutes at 325°F, then reduce heat to 250°F. Continue baking for an additional 40 minutes. Turn off oven; let cheesecake cool in pan for at least 1 hour.
8. Remove from oven and loosen sides of cake with a knife. When completely cooled, refrigerate at least 2 hours, or overnight.
9. Beat remaining cream cheese with orange juice until smooth. Gradually fold in powdered sugar until well mixed. Spread over chilled cheesecake. Garnish with extra raisins and finely shredded carrots.
